title: Implications: Atlas as a Category

category.object: EML Atlas as a category AtlasEML

structure.morphisms: Depth-preserving maps f: D₁ → D₂ with d(f(X)) ≤ d(X)
structure.composition: f∘g: d(f∘g(X)) ≤ max(d(f), d(g)) — tropical semiring ✓
structure.why: Category = EML-0 (small category); with metric = EML-2

functor_laws.composition: d(f∘g) = d(f) + d(g): additive on depth changes ✓

physics_to_math.G: Same domain via different EML formulation

depth_reduction.depth: η: F(EML-3) ⇒ G(EML-2): cross-type = EML-∞ (not natural in standard sense)

nat_trans.insight: Natural transformations between same-depth functors: depth preserved. Cross-depth: EML-∞

shadow_adjoint.significance: Shadow Depth Theorem = existence of right adjoint to categorification

EFT_adjoint.R: IR reduction: EML-3 → EML-2 (EFT matching, S311)

subobject_classifier.\u03a9: Truth values for depth predicates: Ω = {EML-0, EML-1, EML-2, EML-3, EML-∞}
subobject_classifier.why: Ω = finite set = EML-0; with metric structure = EML-2

analysis.internal_logic: Internal logic of AtlasEML = tropical logic (S310)
analysis.reason: ∞-topos of all domains: EML-∞; shadow = {2,3} (by S287 result)

verdicts.shadow_adjunction: Shadow Depth Theorem = right adjoint to categorification
verdicts.new_theorem: Shadow Depth Theorem = existence of right adjoint R: Categorification ⊣ Shadow

key_theorem: Categorical Atlas Theorem (S313): AtlasEML is a category with depth-preserving morphisms and depth-additive composition. Depth functor d: AtlasEML → DepthSemiring is EML-2 (S271 fixed point). NEW: SHADOW DEPTH THEOREM = ADJUNCTION. Categorification (EML-k → EML-∞) is the LEFT adjoint. Shadow (EML-∞ → EML-{2,3}) is the RIGHT adjoint. Shadow Depth Theorem ≡ existence of this right adjoint. EFT matching (S311) = another adjunction: UV ⊣ IR (EML-3 ⊣ EML-2). Natural transformations between same-depth functors: natural (depth preserved). Cross-depth natural transformations: EML-∞. AtlasEML as ∞-topos: EML-∞, shadow={2,3}.
rabbit_hole_log: AtlasEML: category with depth-preserving morphisms and additive composition
Depth functor: EML-2 (additive; same S271 fixed point)
NEW: Shadow Depth Theorem = right adjoint to categorification
Categorification ⊣ Shadow: adjunction explains why shadow ∈ {2,3}
